seoTitle: Chum Creek vs Churchill — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Chum Creek and Churchill. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Chum Creek vs Churchill: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Chum Creek and Churchill.
heading: Chum Creek vs Churchill — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Chum Creek, Victoria and Churchill, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Chum Creek has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Churchill (34/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Chum Creek or Churchill safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Chum Creek has a flood risk score of 24/100, while Churchill scores 31/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Chum Creek scores 30/100 for bushfire risk. Churchill scores 42/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Chum Creek or Churchill?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Chum Creek has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.65% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
